response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack software craftsman with strong web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, performance architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no evidence of GPU/accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD work, DNN training/inference optimization at the hardware level, or the advanced degree typically expected. The skill set is fundamentally misaligned with a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Broad multi-language engineering experience including C/C++ and DLang
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, teaching)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or DNN inference/training optimization background
Education and experience profile does not match Principal-level hardware performance role

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN optimization background
Some low-level C/C++ and performance-sensitive systems exposure
